blobStore.description:
Where the raw content of email messages, attachments, and other large files is stored.
Leave as default to reuse the data store, or point to an object storage service such as S3 for larger deployments.

dataStore.description:
Where structured data is kept: email metadata, calendars, contacts, mailbox state, and server settings.
RocksDB is recommended for single-node installations; PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and FoundationDB are also supported.

defaultDomain.description:
The primary email domain this installation will serve, for example example.com.
Additional domains can be added at any time after setup.

directory.description:
Where user accounts and credentials come from.
The internal directory is recommended for ease of setup and management through the WebUI, but external OIDC or LDAP directories can be used for single sign-on and user provisioning in larger organizations.

dnsServer.description:
Optionally automate the DNS records your mail server needs (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and more) by connecting to your DNS provider's API.
Leave as manual unless your DNS is hosted by a supported provider; this can always be enabled later.

generateDkimKeys.description:
Generate DKIM signing keys for the default domain. DKIM cryptographically signs outgoing mail and significantly improves the chances that messages reach the recipient's inbox instead of spam.
Turn this off only if you plan to manage DKIM keys yourself.

inMemoryStore.description:
Where short-lived data lives: session caches, rate-limit counters, and temporary tokens.
Leave as default to reuse the data store, or point to Redis for faster lookups and multi-node deployments.

requestTlsCertificate.description:
Automatically obtain a free TLS certificate for the server hostname from Let's Encrypt using the ACME protocol, so clients can connect securely out of the box.
Turn this off if you plan to install a certificate manually.

searchStore.description:
Where the full-text search index is kept, so users can search across message bodies and attachments.
Leave as default to reuse the data store, or point to a dedicated search backend for larger deployments.

secret.description: The password for the administrator account, generated by the server

serverHostname.description:
The public hostname this server answers to, for example mail.example.com.
Used in SMTP greetings, outgoing message headers, and TLS certificate requests.

tracer.description:
Where the server writes log messages, traces, and diagnostic events.
Defaults to log files on disk; remote destinations such as OpenTelemetry or webhooks can be added after setup.

username.description: The email address of administrator, generated by the server

subjectAlternativeNames.description:
Additional hostnames to include in the certificate as Subject Alternative Names (SANs).
Enter hostnames only (e.g. `mta-sts`, `autoconfig`), the domain is appended automatically.
To include the apex domain, enter it in full (e.g. `example.org`).
Leave empty to request a wildcard certificate when possible, or to use the default SANs.

archiveDeletedAccountsFor.description: How long to keep deleted accounts in the archive before they are permanently deleted. If null, deleted accounts will be permanently deleted immediately.

archiveDeletedItemsFor.description: How long to keep deleted items in the archive before they are permanently deleted. If null, deleted items will not be archived and will be permanently deleted immediately.

blobCleanupSchedule.description: How often to purge the data store. Expects a cron expression.

dataCleanupSchedule.description: How often to purge the data store. Expects a cron expression.

expungeSchedule.description: Specifies when to run the auto-expunge process

expungeSchedulingInboxAfter.description: Sets the duration after which the iTIP inbox will automatically expunge old messages.

expungeShareNotifyAfter.description: Specifies the duration for which the JMAP share notification history is retained before it is automatically purged.

expungeSubmissionsAfter.description: How long to keep sent e-mail submissions before auto-expunging

expungeTrashAfter.description: How long to keep messages in the Trash and Junk Mail folders before auto-expunging

holdMetricsFor.description: How long to keep metrics history before it is permanently deleted.

holdTracesFor.description: How long to keep message delivery history before it is permanently deleted.

maxChangesHistory.description: How many changes to keep in the history for each account. This is used to determine the changes that have occurred since the last time the client requested changes.

deleteAfter.description: How long to retain old DKIM keys on the server after rotation before deleting them permanently. Requires automatic DNS management.

selectorTemplate.description:
Template for generating DKIM selectors during key rotation. Supported variables:
- `{algorithm}`: signing algorithm in lowercase (`rsa`, `ed25519`)
- `{hash}`: hash algorithm (`sha256`)
- `{version}`: DKIM version number (`1`)
- `{date-<fmt>}`: current UTC date formatted with chrono strftime (e.g. `{date-%Y%m%d}`)
- `{epoch}`: current UTC unix timestamp
- `{random}`: random 8-character alphanumeric string

claimGroups.description: The claim name used to retrieve the user's group memberships from the token or user info response. Common values are groups or roles depending on your provider's configuration. If not set, group information will not be populated. Note that some providers omit group claims from the token to keep its size small and only return them via the user info endpoint, if group information is missing, ensure your provider is configured to include it.

claimUsername.description: The claim name used to retrieve the user's login name from the token or user info response. Common values are preferred_username, email, or sub depending on your provider's configuration. If the claim value is not an email address and usernameDomain is set, the domain will be appended automatically (e.g. john becomes john@example.com). If the claim value already contains an @, it is used as-is. If the claim value is not an email address and no usernameDomain is configured, Stalwart will fall back to the email claim. If neither yields a valid email address, authentication will be rejected.

EventType.name: acme.auth-start
EventType.label: ACME authentication started
EventType.explanation: The domain ownership validation challenge has begun; the server will attempt to prove control of the domain to the ACME provider.

EventType.name: acme.auth-pending
EventType.label: ACME authentication pending
EventType.explanation: The ACME server has acknowledged the challenge but validation is not yet complete; the server is polling for the result.

EventType.name: acme.auth-valid
EventType.label: ACME authentication valid
EventType.explanation: The ACME server confirmed that the domain ownership challenge was successfully validated.

EventType.name: acme.auth-completed
EventType.label: ACME authentication completed
EventType.explanation: Domain ownership validation has finished and the authorization is now in a terminal state.

EventType.name: acme.auth-error
EventType.label: ACME authentication error
EventType.explanation: The domain ownership challenge failed; certificate renewal cannot proceed until the underlying DNS or network issue is resolved.

EventType.name: acme.auth-too-many-attempts
EventType.label: Too many ACME authentication attempts
EventType.explanation: The ACME provider rejected the request because too many validation attempts have been made for this domain in a short period.

EventType.name: acme.process-cert
EventType.label: Processing ACME certificate
EventType.explanation: A newly issued certificate has been received from the ACME provider and is being installed into the TLS configuration.

EventType.name: acme.order-start
EventType.label: ACME order started
EventType.explanation: A new certificate order has been submitted to the ACME provider; domain ownership challenges will follow.

EventType.name: acme.order-processing
EventType.label: ACME order processing
EventType.explanation: The ACME provider is processing the order after all challenges passed; the server is waiting for the certificate to be issued.

EventType.name: acme.order-completed
EventType.label: ACME order completed
EventType.explanation: The ACME provider has issued the certificate and it has been successfully installed.

EventType.name: acme.order-ready
EventType.label: ACME order ready
EventType.explanation: All domain ownership challenges have been validated and the order is ready to be finalized.

EventType.name: acme.order-valid
EventType.label: ACME order valid
EventType.explanation: The ACME order reached the "valid" state, meaning the certificate has been issued and is ready for download.

EventType.name: acme.order-invalid
EventType.label: ACME order invalid
EventType.explanation: The ACME order failed and cannot be completed; one or more domain ownership challenges were rejected by the provider.

EventType.name: acme.renew-backoff
EventType.label: ACME renew backoff
EventType.explanation: The ACME server returned HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header; renewal is paused and will be retried after the indicated delay.

EventType.name: acme.client-supplied-sni
EventType.label: ACME client supplied SNI
EventType.explanation: During TLS-ALPN-01 challenge validation, the connecting ACME client provided an SNI hostname matching the domain being validated.

EventType.name: acme.client-missing-sni
EventType.label: ACME client missing SNI
EventType.explanation: During TLS-ALPN-01 challenge validation, the ACME client did not supply an SNI hostname; the challenge cannot be completed.

EventType.name: acme.tls-alpn-received
EventType.label: ACME TLS ALPN received
EventType.explanation: A TLS-ALPN-01 challenge connection was received from the ACME provider and is being processed.

EventType.name: acme.tls-alpn-error
EventType.label: ACME TLS ALPN error
EventType.explanation: The TLS-ALPN-01 challenge validation failed; check that port 443 is reachable and that no other service is intercepting the connection.

EventType.name: acme.token-not-found
EventType.label: ACME token not found
EventType.explanation: The HTTP-01 challenge token requested by the ACME provider was not found in the token store; the challenge will fail.

EventType.name: acme.error
EventType.label: ACME error
EventType.explanation: An unrecoverable error occurred during ACME certificate management; automatic certificate renewal may be interrupted.

EventType.name: ai.llm-response
EventType.label: LLM response
EventType.explanation: A response was received from the configured AI/LLM provider; logged at trace level for debugging AI-assisted classification or generation.

EventType.name: ai.api-error
EventType.label: AI API error
EventType.explanation: A request to the AI provider API failed; features relying on LLM assistance (e.g. smart spam classification) may be degraded.

EventType.name: arc.chain-too-long
EventType.label: ARC chain too long
EventType.explanation: The message contains more than 50 ARC header sets, exceeding the maximum allowed chain length; ARC verification will fail.

EventType.name: arc.invalid-instance
EventType.label: Invalid ARC instance
EventType.explanation: An ARC header set has an instance number that is out of sequence or otherwise invalid, breaking the chain integrity.

EventType.name: arc.invalid-cv
EventType.label: Invalid ARC CV
EventType.explanation: The ARC chain validation (cv=) tag contains an invalid or contradictory value; the chain cannot be trusted.

EventType.name: arc.has-header-tag
EventType.label: ARC has header tag
EventType.explanation: An ARC-Seal header contains an unexpected header tag, which violates the ARC specification.

EventType.name: arc.broken-chain
EventType.label: Broken ARC chain
EventType.explanation: One or more ARC signatures in the chain failed verification; forwarded messages from this path cannot be trusted.

EventType.name: arc.sealer-not-found
EventType.label: ARC sealer not found
EventType.explanation: The ARC sealer referenced in configuration was not found; outbound ARC signing will be skipped for affected messages.

EventType.name: auth.success
EventType.label: Authentication successful
EventType.explanation: A user or service successfully authenticated; credentials were verified against the configured directory or OAuth provider.

EventType.name: auth.failed
EventType.label: Authentication failed
EventType.explanation: A login attempt was rejected due to incorrect credentials; repeated failures from the same IP may trigger an authentication ban.

EventType.name: auth.token-expired
EventType.label: OAuth token expired
EventType.explanation: The OAuth access or refresh token presented by the client has expired and must be renewed before the session can continue.

EventType.name: auth.mfa-required
EventType.label: Missing MFA token for authentication
EventType.explanation: MFA token is required for authentication but was not provided

EventType.name: auth.too-many-attempts
EventType.label: Too many authentication attempts
EventType.explanation: The client has exceeded the configured limit for failed login attempts in a rolling window; further attempts may be blocked.

EventType.name: auth.client-registration
EventType.label: OAuth Client registration
EventType.explanation: A new OAuth client application was successfully registered with the server's OAuth provider.

EventType.name: auth.error
EventType.label: Authentication error
EventType.explanation: An internal error occurred while processing an authentication request; the login cannot proceed.

EventType.name: auth.warning
EventType.label: Authentication warning
EventType.explanation: A non-fatal issue was detected during authentication processing, such as an unexpected token format or directory lookup anomaly.

EventType.name: auth.credential-expired
EventType.label: Credential expired
EventType.explanation: The credential (password, token, or certificate) used for authentication has passed its expiry date.

EventType.name: calendar.rule-expansion-error
EventType.label: Calendar rule expansion error
EventType.explanation: Failed to expand a recurring calendar event's recurrence rule into concrete instances; affected occurrences may not appear in the user's calendar.

EventType.name: calendar.alarm-sent
EventType.label: Calendar alarm sent
EventType.explanation: A calendar event alarm notification was successfully dispatched to the recipient via email or push.

EventType.name: calendar.alarm-skipped
EventType.label: Calendar alarm skipped
EventType.explanation: A calendar alarm was not sent because the trigger time was missed, the event was already acknowledged, or the recipient is unavailable.

EventType.name: calendar.alarm-recipient-override
EventType.label: Calendar alarm recipient overriden
EventType.explanation: The alarm recipient was substituted by a server-configured override rather than the address specified in the calendar event.

EventType.name: calendar.alarm-failed
EventType.label: Calendar alarm could not be sent
EventType.explanation: Delivery of a calendar alarm notification failed; the recipient may not be notified of the upcoming event.

EventType.name: calendar.itip-message-sent
EventType.label: Calendar iTIP message sent
EventType.explanation: A scheduling message (invite, update, cancellation, or reply) was sent to an external calendar participant via email.

EventType.name: calendar.itip-message-received
EventType.label: Calendar iTIP message received
EventType.explanation: An inbound iTIP/iMIP scheduling message was received and is being processed to update the recipient's calendar.

EventType.name: calendar.itip-message-error
EventType.label: iTIP message error
EventType.explanation: An iTIP/iMIP scheduling message could not be parsed or applied; the calendar event may not be updated correctly.

EventType.name: cluster.startup
EventType.label: Clustering enabled
EventType.explanation: The clustering subsystem has been initialized; inter-node communication via the configured PubSub backend (Kafka, NATS, or Redis) is now active.

EventType.name: cluster.subscriber-start
EventType.label: PubSub subscriber started
EventType.explanation: This node has connected to the PubSub backend and begun consuming inter-node messages.

EventType.name: cluster.subscriber-stop
EventType.label: PubSub subscriber stopped
EventType.explanation: This node's PubSub subscription has been torn down, typically during a graceful shutdown.

EventType.name: cluster.subscriber-error
EventType.label: PubSub subscriber error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred on the PubSub subscription channel; inter-node cache invalidation and state sync may be disrupted.

EventType.name: cluster.subscriber-disconnected
EventType.label: PubSub subscriber disconnected
EventType.explanation: The PubSub subscriber lost its connection to the backend; the node will attempt to reconnect automatically.

EventType.name: cluster.publisher-start
EventType.label: PubSub publisher started
EventType.explanation: This node has established a publisher connection to the PubSub backend for broadcasting events to other cluster nodes.

EventType.name: cluster.publisher-stop
EventType.label: PubSub publisher stopped
EventType.explanation: This node's PubSub publisher has been shut down; no further inter-node notifications will be sent.

EventType.name: cluster.publisher-error
EventType.label: PubSub publisher error
EventType.explanation: Failed to publish a message to the PubSub backend; other cluster nodes may not receive this event.

EventType.name: cluster.message-received
EventType.label: PubSub message received
EventType.explanation: An inter-node message was received from the PubSub backend and is being dispatched to the appropriate handler.

EventType.name: cluster.message-skipped
EventType.label: PubSub message skipped
EventType.explanation: A PubSub message was ignored because it originated from this node and would cause a processing loop.

EventType.name: cluster.message-invalid
EventType.label: Invalid PubSub message
EventType.explanation: A message received from the PubSub backend could not be deserialized; it may indicate a version mismatch between cluster nodes.

EventType.name: cluster.node-id-renewed
EventType.label: Node ID renewed
EventType.explanation: This node's distributed lease has been refreshed, confirming it remains the active holder of its node identity in the cluster.

EventType.name: dane.authentication-success
EventType.label: DANE authentication successful
EventType.explanation: The remote server's certificate matched a TLSA record in DNS; the TLS connection is cryptographically verified via DANE.

EventType.name: dane.authentication-failure
EventType.label: DANE authentication failed
EventType.explanation: The remote server's certificate did not match any TLSA record; the connection may be rejected depending on policy.

EventType.name: dane.no-certificates-found
EventType.label: No certificates found for DANE
EventType.explanation: The TLS handshake completed but no certificates were presented by the remote server for TLSA matching.

EventType.name: dane.certificate-parse-error
EventType.label: Error parsing certificate for DANE
EventType.explanation: A certificate presented during the TLS handshake could not be parsed; DANE verification cannot proceed.

EventType.name: dane.tlsa-record-match
EventType.label: TLSA record match found
EventType.explanation: The remote server's certificate matched a TLSA record, confirming the server's identity via DANE.

EventType.name: dane.tlsa-record-fetch
EventType.label: Fetching TLSA record
EventType.explanation: A DNS lookup is being performed to retrieve the TLSA record for the remote server's domain.

EventType.name: dane.tlsa-record-fetch-error
EventType.label: Error fetching TLSA record
EventType.explanation: The DNS lookup for the TLSA record failed; DANE verification cannot be performed for this connection.

EventType.name: dane.tlsa-record-not-found
EventType.label: TLSA record not found
EventType.explanation: No TLSA record exists for the remote server's domain; DANE verification is skipped for this connection.

EventType.name: dane.tlsa-record-not-dnssec-signed
EventType.label: TLSA record not DNSSEC signed
EventType.explanation: The TLSA record was found but is not protected by DNSSEC; it cannot be trusted for DANE verification.

EventType.name: dane.tlsa-record-invalid
EventType.label: Invalid TLSA record
EventType.explanation: The TLSA record syntax or content is invalid and cannot be used for certificate matching.

EventType.name: delivery.attempt-start
EventType.label: Delivery attempt started
EventType.explanation: A new outbound SMTP delivery attempt is beginning; the server will look up MX records and try to connect to the recipient's mail server.

EventType.name: delivery.attempt-end
EventType.label: Delivery attempt ended
EventType.explanation: The outbound delivery attempt has finished, either successfully or with a temporary/permanent failure recorded.

EventType.name: delivery.completed
EventType.label: Delivery completed
EventType.explanation: All recipients for this message have been either delivered or permanently failed; the message is removed from the queue.

EventType.name: delivery.failed
EventType.label: Delivery failed
EventType.explanation: The delivery attempt failed with a temporary error; the message will be rescheduled for a later retry.

EventType.name: delivery.domain-delivery-start
EventType.label: New delivery attempt for domain
EventType.explanation: An outbound delivery attempt is starting for a specific recipient domain; MX lookup and connection will follow.

EventType.name: delivery.mx-lookup
EventType.label: MX record lookup
EventType.explanation: Performing a DNS MX record lookup to determine which mail servers accept mail for the recipient domain.

EventType.name: delivery.mx-lookup-failed
EventType.label: MX record lookup failed
EventType.explanation: DNS returned no MX records or an error for the recipient domain; delivery to this domain cannot proceed.

EventType.name: delivery.ip-lookup
EventType.label: IP address lookup
EventType.explanation: Resolving the IP address of an MX host before attempting a connection.

EventType.name: delivery.ip-lookup-failed
EventType.label: IP address lookup failed
EventType.explanation: DNS resolution of an MX hostname failed; this host will be skipped and the next MX record tried.

EventType.name: delivery.null-mx
EventType.label: Null MX record found
EventType.explanation: The recipient domain published a null MX record (RFC 7505), explicitly indicating it does not accept email; delivery is permanently impossible.

EventType.name: delivery.connect
EventType.label: Connecting to remote server
EventType.explanation: A TCP connection is being established to the remote mail server for outbound delivery.

EventType.name: delivery.connect-error
EventType.label: Connection error
EventType.explanation: The TCP connection to the remote mail server failed; another MX host will be tried if available.

EventType.name: delivery.missing-outbound-hostname
EventType.label: Missing outbound hostname in configuration
EventType.explanation: No outbound EHLO hostname is configured; a hostname is required to present in the EHLO command to remote servers.

EventType.name: delivery.greeting-failed
EventType.label: SMTP greeting failed
EventType.explanation: The remote server did not send a valid SMTP 220 greeting after connection; the session cannot continue.

EventType.name: delivery.ehlo
EventType.label: SMTP EHLO command
EventType.explanation: The EHLO command was sent to the remote server to advertise capabilities and begin the SMTP session.

EventType.name: delivery.ehlo-rejected
EventType.label: SMTP EHLO rejected
EventType.explanation: The remote server rejected the EHLO command; delivery to this host cannot proceed.

EventType.name: delivery.auth
EventType.label: SMTP authentication
EventType.explanation: Authenticating with the remote server using the configured relay credentials.

EventType.name: delivery.auth-failed
EventType.label: SMTP authentication failed
EventType.explanation: The configured relay credentials were rejected by the remote server; delivery via this relay will fail.

EventType.name: delivery.mail-from
EventType.label: SMTP MAIL FROM command
EventType.explanation: The MAIL FROM command was sent to declare the envelope sender address to the remote server.

EventType.name: delivery.mail-from-rejected
EventType.label: SMTP MAIL FROM rejected
EventType.explanation: The remote server rejected the MAIL FROM command; the sender address or domain may be blocked.

EventType.name: delivery.delivered
EventType.label: Message delivered
EventType.explanation: The remote server accepted the message for the recipient with a 250 response; delivery is complete.

EventType.name: delivery.rcpt-to
EventType.label: SMTP RCPT TO command
EventType.explanation: The RCPT TO command was sent to specify the envelope recipient address to the remote server.

EventType.name: delivery.rcpt-to-rejected
EventType.label: SMTP RCPT TO rejected
EventType.explanation: The remote server rejected the recipient address; a DSN will be generated for permanent failures.

EventType.name: delivery.rcpt-to-failed
EventType.label: SMTP RCPT TO failed
EventType.explanation: The RCPT TO command could not be sent due to a connection error; delivery will be retried.

EventType.name: delivery.message-rejected
EventType.label: Message rejected by remote server
EventType.explanation: The remote server rejected the message data after accepting the envelope; a DSN will be generated.

EventType.name: delivery.start-tls
EventType.label: SMTP STARTTLS command
EventType.explanation: Requesting a TLS upgrade on the existing SMTP connection to the remote server.

EventType.name: delivery.start-tls-unavailable
EventType.label: STARTTLS unavailable
EventType.explanation: The remote server does not advertise STARTTLS; the connection will proceed unencrypted if policy allows.

EventType.name: delivery.start-tls-error
EventType.label: STARTTLS error
EventType.explanation: The TLS handshake after STARTTLS failed; the connection may fall back to plaintext or be abandoned depending on policy.

EventType.name: delivery.start-tls-disabled
EventType.label: STARTTLS disabled
EventType.explanation: STARTTLS has been disabled in the configuration for this remote host; the connection will be plaintext.

EventType.name: delivery.implicit-tls-error
EventType.label: Implicit TLS error
EventType.explanation: The TLS handshake on the implicit TLS port (e.g. port 465) failed; the connection cannot be used.

EventType.name: delivery.concurrency-limit-exceeded
EventType.label: Concurrency limit exceeded
EventType.explanation: The configured maximum number of simultaneous outbound connections to this remote host has been reached; delivery is deferred.

EventType.name: delivery.rate-limit-exceeded
EventType.label: Rate limit exceeded
EventType.explanation: The configured outbound message rate limit for this remote host has been reached; delivery is deferred until the window resets.

EventType.name: delivery.double-bounce
EventType.label: Discarding message after double bounce
EventType.explanation: A bounce notification itself bounced; the message is permanently discarded to prevent a delivery loop.

EventType.name: delivery.dsn-success
EventType.label: DSN success notification
EventType.explanation: A delivery success notification (DSN) was generated and queued for the original sender.

EventType.name: delivery.dsn-temp-fail
EventType.label: DSN temporary failure notification
EventType.explanation: A temporary failure delivery status notification was generated; the sender is informed that delivery is being retried.

EventType.name: delivery.dsn-perm-fail
EventType.label: DSN permanent failure notification
EventType.explanation: A permanent failure delivery status notification was generated; the sender is informed that the message cannot be delivered.

EventType.name: delivery.raw-input
EventType.label: Raw SMTP input received
EventType.explanation: Raw bytes received from the remote server; logged at trace level for low-level SMTP session debugging.

EventType.name: delivery.raw-output
EventType.label: Raw SMTP output sent
EventType.explanation: Raw bytes sent to the remote server; logged at trace level for low-level SMTP session debugging.

EventType.name: dkim.pass
EventType.label: DKIM verification passed
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signature on the incoming message was cryptographically valid and the signing domain is authorized.

EventType.name: dkim.neutral
EventType.label: DKIM verification neutral
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signature was present but the result is neither pass nor fail, typically due to a policy setting.

EventType.name: dkim.fail
EventType.label: DKIM verification failed
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signature did not verify; the message may have been tampered with or the key has changed since signing.

EventType.name: dkim.perm-error
EventType.label: DKIM permanent error
EventType.explanation: A permanent DKIM error occurred (e.g. malformed signature or missing required tag); the signature cannot be evaluated.

EventType.name: dkim.temp-error
EventType.label: DKIM temporary error
EventType.explanation: A transient error prevented DKIM verification, such as a DNS timeout when fetching the public key.

EventType.name: dkim.none
EventType.label: No DKIM signature
EventType.explanation: The message carries no DKIM signature; authentication relies on SPF and DMARC alone.

EventType.name: dkim.unsupported-version
EventType.label: Unsupported DKIM version
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signature specifies a version tag that is not supported; the signature is ignored.

EventType.name: dkim.unsupported-algorithm
EventType.label: Unsupported DKIM algorithm
EventType.explanation: The signing algorithm specified in the DKIM signature (e.g. an obsolete or unknown algorithm) is not supported.

EventType.name: dkim.unsupported-canonicalization
EventType.label: Unsupported DKIM canonicalization
EventType.explanation: The canonicalization method in the DKIM signature is not recognized; the signature cannot be verified.

EventType.name: dkim.unsupported-key-type
EventType.label: Unsupported DKIM key type
EventType.explanation: The public key type published in DNS for this DKIM selector is not supported by this server.

EventType.name: dkim.failed-body-hash-match
EventType.label: DKIM body hash mismatch
EventType.explanation: The hash of the message body does not match the body hash in the DKIM signature; the message body was likely modified in transit.

EventType.name: dkim.failed-verification
EventType.label: DKIM verification failed
EventType.explanation: Cryptographic verification of the DKIM signature failed; the signature or the message has been altered.

EventType.name: dkim.failed-auid-match
EventType.label: DKIM AUID mismatch
EventType.explanation: The agent or user identifier (i=) in the DKIM signature does not belong to the signing domain (d=).

EventType.name: dkim.revoked-public-key
EventType.label: DKIM public key revoked
EventType.explanation: The DNS TXT record for the DKIM selector has an empty or revoked key; all signatures from this selector are invalid.

EventType.name: dkim.incompatible-algorithms
EventType.label: Incompatible DKIM algorithms
EventType.explanation: The signature algorithm and the public key type are incompatible with each other; verification cannot proceed.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-expired
EventType.label: DKIM signature expired
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signature's expiry time (x= tag) has passed; the signature is no longer valid.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-length
EventType.label: DKIM signature length issue
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signature's body length limit (l= tag) does not match the actual message body length.

EventType.name: dkim.signer-not-found
EventType.label: DKIM signer not found
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signing key referenced in configuration was not found; outbound messages will not be signed.

EventType.name: dkim.build-error
EventType.label: DKIM build error
EventType.explanation: Failed to construct a DKIM signature due to a configuration or key loading error.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-created
EventType.label: DKIM signature created
EventType.explanation: A new DKIM signing key pair has been generated and stored, ready for publication to DNS.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-published
EventType.label: DKIM signature published
EventType.explanation: The DKIM public key has been published as a DNS TXT record and is now active for verification by recipients.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-retiring
EventType.label: DKIM signature retiring
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signing key is being phased out; the DNS record will be removed after the retirement period to allow in-flight messages to verify.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-retired
EventType.label: DKIM signature retired
EventType.explanation: The DKIM key's retirement period has elapsed and its DNS record has been removed; the key is no longer in use.

EventType.name: dkim.signature-deleted
EventType.label: DKIM signature deleted
EventType.explanation: The DKIM signing key has been permanently deleted from the system and its DNS record removed.

EventType.name: dmarc.pass
EventType.label: DMARC check passed
EventType.explanation: The message passed DMARC evaluation; either DKIM or SPF aligned with the From domain per the domain's policy.

EventType.name: dmarc.fail
EventType.label: DMARC check failed
EventType.explanation: The message failed DMARC evaluation; neither DKIM nor SPF produced an aligned pass, and the domain's policy may require rejection or quarantine.

EventType.name: dmarc.perm-error
EventType.label: DMARC permanent error
EventType.explanation: A permanent error prevented DMARC evaluation, such as a malformed policy record; the check result is indeterminate.

EventType.name: dmarc.temp-error
EventType.label: DMARC temporary error
EventType.explanation: A transient error prevented DMARC evaluation, such as a DNS timeout when fetching the policy record; the check will not be retried for this message.

EventType.name: dmarc.none
EventType.label: No DMARC record
EventType.explanation: No DMARC policy record was found for the From domain; the message is not subject to DMARC enforcement.

EventType.name: dns.record-created
EventType.label: DNS record created
EventType.explanation: The DNS provider API accepted the new record; propagation polling will begin to confirm visibility.

EventType.name: dns.record-creation-failed
EventType.label: DNS record creation failed
EventType.explanation: The DNS provider API rejected or failed the record creation request; ACME DNS-01 challenges or DKIM publishing may be blocked.

EventType.name: dns.record-deletion-failed
EventType.label: DNS record deletion failed
EventType.explanation: The DNS provider API could not remove the record; stale challenge or DKIM records may remain in DNS.

EventType.name: dns.record-not-propagated
EventType.label: DNS record not propagated
EventType.explanation: A poll of the authoritative DNS servers shows the record has not yet appeared; the server will retry until the propagation timeout.

EventType.name: dns.record-lookup-failed
EventType.label: DNS record lookup failed
EventType.explanation: A DNS lookup performed during propagation polling returned an error; check DNS resolver connectivity.

EventType.name: dns.record-propagated
EventType.label: DNS record propagated
EventType.explanation: The DNS record is now visible on the authoritative nameservers; propagation polling has completed successfully.

EventType.name: dns.record-propagation-timeout
EventType.label: DNS record propagation timeout
EventType.explanation: The configured propagation timeout elapsed before the record became visible; the dependent operation (e.g. ACME challenge) may fail.

EventType.name: dns.build-error
EventType.label: DNS updater build error
EventType.explanation: Failed to initialize the DNS provider client from configuration; automatic DNS record management will not function.

EventType.name: eval.result
EventType.label: Expression evaluation result
EventType.explanation: A configuration expression was evaluated and produced a result; logged at trace level for debugging rule logic.

EventType.name: eval.error
EventType.label: Expression evaluation error
EventType.explanation: A configuration expression could not be evaluated due to a syntax or runtime error; the rule using this expression may not behave as expected.

EventType.name: eval.directory-not-found
EventType.label: Directory not found while evaluating expression
EventType.explanation: An expression referenced a directory backend by name that does not exist in the configuration; the expression cannot be evaluated.

EventType.name: eval.store-not-found
EventType.label: Store not found while evaluating expression
EventType.explanation: An expression referenced a store backend by name that does not exist in the configuration; the expression cannot be evaluated.

EventType.name: http.connection-start
EventType.label: HTTP connection started
EventType.explanation: A new inbound HTTP/HTTPS connection was accepted by the server.

EventType.name: http.connection-end
EventType.label: HTTP connection ended
EventType.explanation: An inbound HTTP/HTTPS connection was closed after completing its requests.

EventType.name: http.error
EventType.label: HTTP error occurred
EventType.explanation: An error was returned during an HTTP request, such as a 4xx or 5xx response or a transport-level failure.

EventType.name: http.request-url
EventType.label: HTTP request URL
EventType.explanation: Logs the URL of an incoming HTTP request for debugging purposes.

EventType.name: http.request-body
EventType.label: HTTP request body
EventType.explanation: Logs the full body of an incoming HTTP request; only emitted at trace level due to potentially sensitive content.

EventType.name: http.response-body
EventType.label: HTTP response body
EventType.explanation: Logs the full body of an outgoing HTTP response; only emitted at trace level due to potentially sensitive content.

EventType.name: http.x-forwarded-missing
EventType.label: X-Forwarded-For header is missing
EventType.explanation: The server is configured to trust a reverse proxy but the X-Forwarded-For header was absent; the client IP cannot be determined accurately.

EventType.name: imap.connection-start
EventType.label: IMAP connection started
EventType.explanation: A new IMAP client connection was accepted; the server will begin processing IMAP commands from this client.

EventType.name: imap.connection-end
EventType.label: IMAP connection ended
EventType.explanation: An IMAP client connection was closed, either by the client logging out or due to a timeout or error.

EventType.name: imap.get-acl
EventType.label: IMAP GET ACL command
EventType.explanation: Client requested mailbox ACL

EventType.name: imap.set-acl
EventType.label: IMAP SET ACL command
EventType.explanation: Client set mailbox ACL

EventType.name: imap.my-rights
EventType.label: IMAP MYRIGHTS command
EventType.explanation: Client requested mailbox rights

EventType.name: imap.list-rights
EventType.label: IMAP LISTRIGHTS command
EventType.explanation: Client requested mailbox rights list

EventType.name: imap.append
EventType.label: IMAP APPEND command
EventType.explanation: Client appended a message to a mailbox

EventType.name: imap.capabilities
EventType.label: IMAP CAPABILITIES command
EventType.explanation: Client requested server capabilities

EventType.name: imap.id
EventType.label: IMAP ID command
EventType.explanation: Client sent an ID command

EventType.name: imap.close
EventType.label: IMAP CLOSE command
EventType.explanation: Client closed a mailbox

EventType.name: imap.copy
EventType.label: IMAP COPY command
EventType.explanation: Client copied messages between mailboxes

EventType.name: imap.move
EventType.label: IMAP MOVE command
EventType.explanation: Client moved messages between mailboxes

EventType.name: imap.create-mailbox
EventType.label: IMAP CREATE mailbox command
EventType.explanation: Client created a mailbox

EventType.name: imap.delete-mailbox

EventType.name: incoming-report.dmarc-report
EventType.label: DMARC report received
EventType.explanation: An aggregate DMARC report was received from a remote mail server and successfully parsed for processing.

EventType.name: incoming-report.dmarc-report-with-warnings
EventType.label: DMARC report received with warnings
EventType.explanation: A DMARC aggregate report was received and parsed but contained non-fatal issues; some data may have been skipped.

EventType.name: incoming-report.tls-report
EventType.label: TLS report received
EventType.explanation: A TLS-RPT report was received from a remote mail server and successfully parsed for processing.

EventType.name: incoming-report.tls-report-with-warnings
EventType.label: TLS report received with warnings
EventType.explanation: A TLS-RPT report was received and parsed but contained non-fatal issues; some data may have been skipped.

EventType.name: incoming-report.abuse-report
EventType.label: Abuse report received
EventType.explanation: An ARF abuse report (e.g. spam complaint) was received from a feedback loop provider.

EventType.name: incoming-report.auth-failure-report
EventType.label: Authentication failure report received
EventType.explanation: An ARF authentication failure report was received, indicating a message failed DKIM, SPF, or DMARC at a remote site.

EventType.name: incoming-report.fraud-report
EventType.label: Fraud report received
EventType.explanation: An ARF fraud report was received, indicating a message was identified as phishing or a scam at the remote site.

EventType.name: incoming-report.not-spam-report
EventType.label: Not spam report received
EventType.explanation: An ARF not-spam report was received, indicating a false-positive spam classification by the remote site.

EventType.name: incoming-report.virus-report
EventType.label: Virus report received
EventType.explanation: An ARF virus report was received, indicating a message was identified as containing malware at the remote site.

EventType.name: incoming-report.other-report
EventType.label: Other type of report received
EventType.explanation: An ARF report of an unrecognized type was received; it was logged but could not be classified for processing.

EventType.name: incoming-report.message-parse-failed
EventType.label: Failed to parse incoming report message
EventType.explanation: The outer email message containing the report could not be parsed; the report contents cannot be processed.

EventType.name: incoming-report.dmarc-parse-failed
EventType.label: Failed to parse DMARC report
EventType.explanation: The XML payload of the DMARC aggregate report could not be parsed; the report data will not be recorded.

EventType.name: incoming-report.tls-rpc-parse-failed
EventType.label: Failed to parse TLS RPC report
EventType.explanation: The JSON payload of the TLS-RPT report could not be parsed; the report data will not be recorded.

EventType.name: incoming-report.arf-parse-failed
EventType.label: Failed to parse ARF report
EventType.explanation: The ARF (Abuse Reporting Format) feedback report could not be parsed; the complaint data will not be recorded.

EventType.name: incoming-report.decompress-error
EventType.label: Error decompressing report
EventType.explanation: The report attachment could not be decompressed (e.g. corrupt gzip); the report contents cannot be processed.

EventType.name: iprev.pass
EventType.label: IPREV check passed
EventType.explanation: The connecting IP's reverse DNS resolved to a hostname that in turn resolved back to the same IP, confirming a matching forward-confirmed reverse DNS.

EventType.name: iprev.fail
EventType.label: IPREV check failed
EventType.explanation: The connecting IP either has no reverse DNS or the forward lookup of that hostname did not resolve back to the original IP.

EventType.name: iprev.perm-error
EventType.label: IPREV permanent error
EventType.explanation: A permanent DNS error prevented the reverse lookup from completing; the IPREV result is indeterminate.

EventType.name: iprev.temp-error
EventType.label: IPREV temporary error
EventType.explanation: A transient DNS error prevented the reverse lookup from completing; the IPREV result is indeterminate for this message.

EventType.name: iprev.none
EventType.label: No IPREV record
EventType.explanation: No PTR record exists for the connecting IP address; IPREV cannot be evaluated.

EventType.name: jmap.method-call
EventType.label: JMAP method call
EventType.explanation: A JMAP API method was invoked by a client; logged for debugging API usage and performance.

EventType.name: jmap.invalid-arguments
EventType.label: Invalid JMAP arguments
EventType.explanation: The client supplied arguments that do not conform to the JMAP method specification; the call is rejected with an invalidArguments error.

EventType.name: jmap.request-too-large
EventType.label: JMAP request too large
EventType.explanation: The JMAP request body exceeds the server's configured maximum size limit and is rejected.

EventType.name: jmap.state-mismatch
EventType.label: JMAP state mismatch
EventType.explanation: The client's ifInState or ifNotInState condition was not satisfied; the operation is rejected to prevent conflicting changes.

EventType.name: jmap.anchor-not-found
EventType.label: JMAP anchor not found
EventType.explanation: The anchor object ID referenced in a paginated query was not found; the result window cannot be positioned.

EventType.name: jmap.unsupported-filter
EventType.label: Unsupported JMAP filter
EventType.explanation: The client used a filter operator or property that is not supported by this server implementation.

EventType.name: jmap.unsupported-sort
EventType.label: Unsupported JMAP sort
EventType.explanation: The client requested sorting by a property that is not supported by this server implementation.

EventType.name: jmap.unknown-method
EventType.label: Unknown JMAP method
EventType.explanation: The client called a method name that is not recognized; check that the required capability is included in the session.

EventType.name: jmap.invalid-result-reference
EventType.label: Invalid JMAP result reference
EventType.explanation: A back-reference to a previous method result in the same request was invalid or pointed to a non-existent result.

EventType.name: jmap.forbidden
EventType.label: JMAP operation forbidden
EventType.explanation: The authenticated account does not have permission to perform this JMAP operation on the requested resource.

EventType.name: jmap.account-not-found
EventType.label: JMAP account not found
EventType.explanation: The account ID supplied in the JMAP request does not exist or is not accessible to the authenticated user.

EventType.name: jmap.account-not-supported-by-method
EventType.label: JMAP account not supported by method
EventType.explanation: The requested JMAP method is not available for the specified account type.

EventType.name: jmap.account-read-only
EventType.label: JMAP account is read-only
EventType.explanation: The client attempted a write operation on an account that only allows read access.

EventType.name: jmap.not-found
EventType.label: JMAP resource not found
EventType.explanation: The requested object ID does not exist in the account or has been destroyed.

EventType.name: jmap.cannot-calculate-changes
EventType.label: Cannot calculate JMAP changes
EventType.explanation: The server cannot compute the change delta since the client's state token; the client must perform a full resync.

EventType.name: jmap.unknown-data-type
EventType.label: Unknown JMAP data type
EventType.explanation: The client referenced a data type that is not supported or not enabled via the session capabilities.

EventType.name: jmap.unknown-capability
EventType.label: Unknown JMAP capability
EventType.explanation: The request included a capability URI that the server does not recognize.

EventType.name: jmap.not-json
EventType.label: JMAP request is not JSON
EventType.explanation: The request body could not be parsed as JSON; the entire JMAP request is rejected.

EventType.name: jmap.not-request
EventType.label: JMAP input is not a request
EventType.explanation: The JSON body was valid but did not conform to the JMAP Request object structure.

EventType.name: jmap.websocket-start
EventType.label: JMAP WebSocket connection started
EventType.explanation: A client established a JMAP-over-WebSocket connection for push updates and multiplexed API calls.

EventType.name: jmap.websocket-stop
EventType.label: JMAP WebSocket connection stopped
EventType.explanation: A JMAP WebSocket connection was closed by the client or due to a timeout.

EventType.name: jmap.websocket-error
EventType.label: JMAP WebSocket error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred on a JMAP WebSocket connection; the connection may have been terminated.

EventType.name: limit.size-request
EventType.label: Request size limit reached
EventType.explanation: The incoming request body exceeds the configured maximum request size; the request is rejected.

EventType.name: limit.size-upload
EventType.label: Upload size limit reached
EventType.explanation: The uploaded blob exceeds the configured maximum upload size; the upload is rejected.

EventType.name: limit.calls-in
EventType.label: Incoming calls limit reached
EventType.explanation: The number of method calls in a single JMAP request exceeds the server's configured maximum.

EventType.name: limit.concurrent-request
EventType.label: Concurrent request limit reached
EventType.explanation: The number of simultaneous in-flight requests from this account or IP has reached the configured limit; the request is rejected.

EventType.name: limit.concurrent-upload
EventType.label: Concurrent upload limit reached
EventType.explanation: The number of simultaneous blob uploads from this account has reached the configured limit.

EventType.name: limit.concurrent-connection
EventType.label: Concurrent connection limit reached
EventType.explanation: The number of simultaneous connections from this IP or account has reached the configured limit; new connections are refused.

EventType.name: limit.quota
EventType.label: Quota limit reached
EventType.explanation: The account's storage quota has been reached; new messages or objects cannot be stored until space is freed.

EventType.name: limit.blob-quota
EventType.label: Blob quota limit reached
EventType.explanation: The account's blob storage quota has been reached; blob uploads will be rejected until space is freed.

EventType.name: limit.tenant-quota
EventType.label: Tenant quota limit reached
EventType.explanation: A tenant-level quota (users, domains, or storage) has been reached; operations that would exceed it are being rejected.

EventType.name: limit.too-many-requests
EventType.label: Too many requests
EventType.explanation: The client has exceeded the configured request rate limit; requests are being throttled until the window resets.

EventType.name: mail-auth.parse-error
EventType.label: Mail authentication parse error
EventType.explanation: A DKIM, SPF, or ARC header could not be parsed; the affected authentication check will be skipped for this message.

EventType.name: mail-auth.missing-parameters
EventType.label: Missing mail authentication parameters
EventType.explanation: A required parameter for mail authentication (e.g. a mandatory DKIM tag) was absent; the check cannot proceed.

EventType.name: mail-auth.no-headers-found
EventType.label: No headers found in message
EventType.explanation: The message contains no headers relevant to mail authentication; signing or verification cannot be performed.

EventType.name: mail-auth.crypto
EventType.label: Crypto error during mail authentication
EventType.explanation: A cryptographic operation (signing or verification) failed during mail authentication processing.

EventType.name: mail-auth.io
EventType.label: I/O error during mail authentication
EventType.explanation: An I/O error occurred while reading message data for mail authentication processing.

EventType.name: mail-auth.base64
EventType.label: Base64 error during mail authentication
EventType.explanation: A base64-encoded value in a mail authentication header (e.g. a DKIM signature) could not be decoded.

EventType.name: mail-auth.dns-error
EventType.label: DNS error
EventType.explanation: A DNS query required for mail authentication (e.g. fetching a DKIM public key or SPF record) returned an error.

EventType.name: mail-auth.dns-record-not-found
EventType.label: DNS record not found
EventType.explanation: The DNS record needed for mail authentication (e.g. a DKIM key or SPF policy) does not exist.

EventType.name: mail-auth.dns-invalid-record-type
EventType.label: Invalid DNS record type
EventType.explanation: The DNS response for a mail authentication lookup returned an unexpected record type.

EventType.name: mail-auth.policy-not-aligned
EventType.label: Policy not aligned
EventType.explanation: The authenticated domain does not align with the message's From header as required by DMARC policy.

EventType.name: message-ingest.ham

EventType.explanation: An inbound message passed spam checks and was successfully delivered to the recipient's mailbox.

EventType.name: message-ingest.spam
EventType.label: Possible spam message ingested
EventType.explanation: An inbound message was classified as likely spam and delivered to the Junk folder rather than the inbox.

EventType.name: message-ingest.imap-append
EventType.label: Message appended via IMAP
EventType.explanation: A message was directly appended to a mailbox by an IMAP client using the APPEND command.

EventType.name: message-ingest.jmap-append
EventType.label: Message appended via JMAP
EventType.explanation: A message was directly appended to a mailbox via the JMAP Email/import method.

EventType.name: message-ingest.duplicate
EventType.label: Skipping duplicate message
EventType.explanation: An incoming message was detected as a duplicate of one already delivered; it has been discarded to prevent duplicates in the mailbox.

EventType.name: message-ingest.error
EventType.label: Message ingestion error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred while storing an inbound message; the message may not have been delivered to the recipient.

EventType.name: message-ingest.search-index
EventType.label: Search index updated
EventType.explanation: The full-text search index was updated after a new message was ingested or an existing message was modified.

EventType.name: milter.read
EventType.label: Reading from Milter
EventType.explanation: Raw data was read from the Milter socket; logged at trace level for low-level Milter protocol debugging.

EventType.name: milter.write

EventType.explanation: Raw data was written to the Milter socket; logged at trace level for low-level Milter protocol debugging.

EventType.name: milter.action-discard
EventType.label: Milter action: Discard
EventType.explanation: The Milter requested to discard the message

EventType.name: milter.action-reject

EventType.explanation: The Milter requested to reject the message

EventType.name: milter.action-temp-fail

EventType.name: milter.action-reply-code
EventType.label: Milter action: Reply code
EventType.explanation: The Milter requested a reply code

EventType.name: milter.action-connection-failure
EventType.label: Milter action: Connection failure
EventType.explanation: The Milter requested a connection failure

EventType.name: milter.action-shutdown

EventType.explanation: The Milter requested a shutdown

EventType.name: milter.frame-too-large

EventType.name: milter.frame-invalid

EventType.name: milter.unexpected-response

EventType.name: milter.tls-invalid-name

EventType.name: mta-hook.action-accept
EventType.label: MTA hook action: Accept

EventType.name: mta-hook.action-discard

EventType.explanation: The MTA hook requested to discard the message

EventType.name: mta-sts.authorized
EventType.label: Host authorized by MTA-STS policy
EventType.explanation: The remote mail server's hostname matches the MTA-STS policy for the recipient domain; the TLS connection is permitted.

EventType.name: mta-sts.not-authorized
EventType.label: Host not authorized by MTA-STS policy
EventType.explanation: The remote mail server's hostname is not listed in the MTA-STS policy; delivery may be blocked depending on the policy mode.

EventType.name: mta-sts.policy-fetch
EventType.label: Fetched MTA-STS policy
EventType.explanation: The MTA-STS policy for the recipient domain was successfully retrieved and cached for outbound delivery decisions.

EventType.name: mta-sts.policy-not-found
EventType.label: MTA-STS policy not found
EventType.explanation: No MTA-STS policy exists for this recipient domain; TLS enforcement via MTA-STS is not applied.

EventType.name: mta-sts.policy-fetch-error
EventType.label: Error fetching MTA-STS policy
EventType.explanation: The MTA-STS policy could not be retrieved from the domain's HTTPS endpoint; TLS enforcement may fall back to best-effort.

EventType.name: mta-sts.invalid-policy
EventType.label: Invalid MTA-STS policy
EventType.explanation: The retrieved MTA-STS policy file is malformed or contains invalid directives; it cannot be applied.

EventType.name: network.listen-start
EventType.label: Network listener started
EventType.explanation: A network listener is now accepting connections on the configured address and port.

EventType.name: network.listen-stop
EventType.label: Network listener stopped
EventType.explanation: A network listener has been shut down and is no longer accepting new connections.

EventType.name: network.listen-error
EventType.label: Network listener error
EventType.explanation: The network listener encountered a fatal error and may have stopped accepting connections.

EventType.name: network.bind-error
EventType.label: Network bind error
EventType.explanation: Failed to bind the listener to the configured address and port; the port may already be in use or permissions may be insufficient.

EventType.name: network.read-error
EventType.label: Network read error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred while reading data from a client connection; the connection may have been dropped by the client.

EventType.name: network.write-error
EventType.label: Network write error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred while writing data to a client connection; the connection may have been closed unexpectedly.

EventType.name: network.flush-error
EventType.label: Network flush error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred while flushing buffered data to a client connection.

EventType.name: network.accept-error
EventType.label: Network accept error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred while accepting a new incoming connection; this is often transient and the listener continues running.

EventType.name: network.split-error
EventType.label: Network split error
EventType.explanation: An error occurred while splitting a TLS stream into read and write halves; the connection cannot be used.

EventType.name: network.timeout
EventType.label: Network timeout
EventType.explanation: A network operation exceeded its configured timeout; the connection will be closed.

EventType.name: network.closed
EventType.label: Network connection closed
EventType.explanation: A network connection was closed cleanly after completing its session.

EventType.name: network.proxy-error
EventType.label: Proxy protocol error
EventType.explanation: The PROXY protocol header from the upstream load balancer was missing or malformed; the real client IP cannot be determined.

EventType.name: network.set-opt-error
EventType.label: Network set option error
EventType.explanation: Failed to set a socket option (e.g. TCP keepalive or buffer size) on a connection; the connection may behave unexpectedly.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.spf-report
EventType.label: SPF report sent
EventType.explanation: An SPF failure report was sent to the address specified in the domain's SPF record.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.spf-rate-limited
EventType.label: SPF report rate limited
EventType.explanation: An SPF failure report was suppressed because the reporting rate limit for this domain has been reached.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.dkim-report
EventType.label: DKIM report sent
EventType.explanation: A DKIM failure report was sent to the address specified in the DKIM signature's reporting tag.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.dkim-rate-limited
EventType.label: DKIM report rate limited
EventType.explanation: A DKIM failure report was suppressed because the reporting rate limit for this selector has been reached.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.dmarc-report
EventType.label: DMARC report sent
EventType.explanation: A DMARC failure report was sent to the address specified in the domain's DMARC policy record.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.dmarc-rate-limited
EventType.label: DMARC report rate limited
EventType.explanation: A DMARC failure report was suppressed because the reporting rate limit for this domain has been reached.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.dmarc-aggregate-report
EventType.label: DMARC aggregate is being prepared
EventType.explanation: A DMARC aggregate report is being compiled and will be sent to the domain's rua address at the scheduled interval.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.tls-aggregate
EventType.label: TLS aggregate report is being prepared
EventType.explanation: A TLS-RPT aggregate report is being compiled and will be sent to the domain's TLS reporting address at the scheduled interval.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.http-submission
EventType.label: Report submitted via HTTP
EventType.explanation: The report was submitted to the recipient's reporting endpoint via an HTTP POST request.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.unauthorized-reporting-address
EventType.label: Unauthorized reporting address
EventType.explanation: The external reporting address for this domain is not authorized via a DNS verification record; the report will not be sent.

EventType.name: outgoing-report.reporting-address-validation-error
